schema_version: v0 — migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Cast nodes — an intention that has passed /sorted, been typed to a node-class and bound to that class's gate-set. The universal work unit.
why: Workflow rides on node-class, not on the node's identity, so a ticket carries no domain — an 'inference ticket' and a 'concept-piece ticket' are the same artifact with a different class field. Everybody has tickets. Crucially, the ticket's `state` field IS the pipeline instance: no orchestrator holds a second copy of where the work stands, so the state of the tickets is the state of all things, always (MAP.md 'Gates, not supervisors'). Written when skill:/sorted casts a node.
owner: each ticket has exactly one owner who gates writes to it; the owner alone advances its state through the bound gates (Law 6). The tickets store owns the collection; the emit chokepoint validates records against the template.

template.owner: the one owner who gates writes to this node
template.intention: what this node is FOR (the WHAT)
template.node_class: the bound class (address in node_classes/): skill | concept-piece | code-seam | …
template.state: the pipeline instance — where this node is in its class's gate-set
template.gates: the class's gate-set bound at casting (proof / derivation / signature check-types)
template.falsifier: what would show this node wrong (required to cast)
template.parent: optional — parent node id (children prove before parents)
template.children: array — child node ids spawned to deconstruct this one

notes_on_the_store: A ticket is never terminal: `validated` = best guess still carrying its falsifier and horizon; the back-edge (build → deconstruct) is the universal shape.
A node with no falsifier does not cast (the /sorted completeness gate).
Ownership stays put; what moves phase-to-phase is which peer is invited to act next, through the owner's gate — not a transfer of ownership.
